intro: The biggest real distinction between AWS Amplify and Ubicloud is straightforward: AWS Amplify uses freemium with a paid tier from $15/month, enterprise plan available; Ubicloud uses usage-based pricing, a free tier. AWS Amplify sits at freemium with a paid tier from $15/month, enterprise plan available and carries low lock-in, while Ubicloud sits at usage-based pricing, a free tier and carries low lock-in. AWS Amplify is a fit when you need mobile, teams — typical scenarios include mvp/prototyping speed. Ubicloud is aimed at teams cutting github actions bills or wanting open-source iaas on hetzner, with workloads like open-source aws alternative running on hetzner bare-metal as the common case. The honest trade-off: AWS Amplify is explicitly not for complex custom business logic; Ubicloud is not intended for enterprises needing the full breadth of aws managed services. On cost specifically, pricing model matters more than sticker price — one workload can make AWS Amplify cheaper while a different shape flips the answer to Ubicloud.
quick_take: AWS Amplify is for mobile, teams; Ubicloud is for teams cutting github actions bills or; decide based on cost model and budget.
verdict_a: Choose AWS Amplify if your primary workload is mobile, teams and the free tier plus $15/month paid tier fits your budget.
verdict_b: Choose Ubicloud if your primary workload is teams cutting github actions bills or wanting open-source iaas on hetzner and low switching cost matters.

faq.q: Is AWS Amplify cheaper than Ubicloud?
faq.a: AWS Amplify publishes an entry price of $15/month; Ubicloud's entry price is not in our data, so a definitive comparison is not possible without a quote from Ubicloud.

faq.q: Can I migrate from AWS Amplify to Ubicloud?
faq.a: Migrating from AWS Amplify to Ubicloud is generally feasible because AWS Amplify has low lock-in, so data and configuration are usually portable. Since both tools sit in the same category (Backend-as-a-Service), the conceptual mapping is closer, but you still need to validate feature parity against your specific workload. Plan migration as a project, not a script: inventory integrations, test on non-production workloads, and keep AWS Amplify running in parallel until Ubicloud is verified.

On our 0–5 developer experience scale, AWS Amplify scores higher (4/5 vs 3/5 for Ubicloud). The gap is one point — meaningful but not decisive, since DX is workflow-dependent. Still, score your own tasks: DX is subjective and tied to stack assumptions.

faq.q: Is Ubicloud a good alternative to AWS Amplify?
faq.a: Ubicloud is in the same category as AWS Amplify (Backend-as-a-Service), so it's a reasonable alternative to evaluate. Ubicloud is aimed at teams cutting github actions bills or wanting open-source iaas on hetzner, while AWS Amplify targets mobile, teams — that difference should drive the switch decision. Note that Ubicloud is explicitly not for enterprises needing the full breadth of aws managed services — if that describes your workload, it's not a viable replacement.
